nodes.label: AI for repetitive simple tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for creative or context-dependent tasks
nodes.grounding_span: anything that's relatively deterministic i'm comfortable using ai, otherwise i prefer to do it myself

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as time waster due to misunderstanding
nodes.grounding_span: using ai for the aforementioned code comments or js docs can be a major time saver

nodes.label: AI for client communication
nodes.grounding_span: quickly get an idea of what a client's application is supposed to do by asking ai

nodes.label: Company AI push vs practical utility
nodes.label_negative: Company AI alignment with actual needs
nodes.grounding_span: the company pushes their own ai pretty hard but i don't find it to be very good

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.grounding_span: the use of ai is itself a skill and the degree to which it will take over is likely to be gradual

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: it's usually not completely correct so i make small edits after that

nodes.label: Autonomy and personal control
nodes.grounding_span: i would still prefer to do things how i've described

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: using ai for the aforementioned code comments or js docs can be a major time saver

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: it can be frustrating when it totally misunderstands or misses important context

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI industry's vision
nodes.grounding_span: the company pushes their own ai pretty hard but i don't find it to be very good

nodes.label: Confidence in AI as a key skill
nodes.grounding_span: i'm pretty confident no serious knowledge-based work is at serious risk anytime soon

nodes.label: Selective delegation based on task nature
nodes.grounding_span: i'll often write out all the test steps without comments, then ask for comments for each action

nodes.label: Pragmatic calibration based on task stakes and verifiability
nodes.grounding_span: i tend to just avoid using it as much as possible for this reason
